title: Is Armory Square really so chaotic? We spent 4 hours there Friday, here’s what we saw
summary: Local news investigation examines safety perceptions in Armory Square following a viral Facebook post by police union claiming gang activity and chaos. Two reporters spent 4 hours observing the neighborhood on a Friday night to verify claims disputed by the police chief and deputy mayor, who attributed post-closing incidents to large gatherings rather than organized gang presence.

political_signal.description: Public accountability journalism examining conflicting official narratives about neighborhood safety and police characterization of public disorder. Highlights tension between police union and city leadership over incident framing.

processing_notes: Article references Facebook viral post (600+ likes, 360+ shares) as catalyst for debate - indicates high constituent engagement
Conflict between police union and city leadership (police chief, deputy mayor) signals internal governance tension requiring CIO attention for potential communications/data transparency needs
Specific observation date (July 18, 2025) and methodology (4-hour direct observation) suggests systematic fact-checking approach
No author byline provided despite attribution to Ankit Bandyopadhyay and Ryann Phillips
Article content appears incomplete (ends mid-narrative at 'Watch' prompt)
